description_long: This is a standard urban traffic light mounted on a tall, dark-colored metal pole with a sturdy square concrete base. The main signal housing is rectangular and painted yellow, featuring three circular signal lights arranged vertically, with the bottom light illuminated green. Attached to the pole below the main signal is a smaller yellow box, likely a pedestrian signal or control unit, with a dark display area. The rear of the signal housing is flat and featureless, while the sides reveal the protruding cylindrical signal hoods that shield each light. The entire structure is robust and weather-resistant, designed for outdoor installation at intersections or crosswalks. The materials and build suggest durability and resistance to tampering or environmental wear.
description: A yellow, pole-mounted traffic light with three vertically stacked signal lights and a concrete base, featuring an additional control or pedestrian signal box below the main lights.
description_view_0: Side view showing the three protruding yellow signal hoods and the attached control box below the main lights.
description_view_1: Rear view displaying the flat back of the yellow signal housing and the side of the control box, with the pole and base fully visible.
description_view_2: Opposite side view highlighting the three cylindrical signal hoods and the control box, mirroring view 0.
description_view_3: Front view showing the three circular signal lights in a vertical arrangement, with the bottom light illuminated green and the control box visible below.
